column18: GUIDE: ps4_mode: "proband_count"; ps4_mode_rationale: "The ClinGen VCEP gene-specific text for SDHB does not provide any modifications to the PS4 criterion. Therefore, the default guidance from the ACMG/AMP 2015 guidelines is applied. These guidelines describe a proband counting approach as a moderate level of evidence for rare variants when formal case-control studies are not available or do not reach statistical significance (Richards et al., 2015, Table 3, PS4 Note 2)."
column19: For rare variants without statistically significant case-control data, the observation of the variant in multiple unrelated patients with the same phenotype, and its absence in controls, may be used (Richards et al., 2015, Table 3, PS4 Note 2).

column18: ps4_mode_rationale: The ClinGen Hearing Loss VCEP provides two pathways for PS4: case-control statistical analysis or a proband counting method for autosomal dominant (AD) disorders. The proband counting method is explicitly defined with numeric thresholds for Strong, Moderate, and Supporting strengths, making it the primary specified mode for AD genes like KCNQ4 (PS4 section).
column19:
PS4_Strong: ≥15 probands with the variant (PS4 section).
PS4_Moderate: ≥6 probands with the variant (PS4 section).
PS4_Supporting: ≥2 probands with the variant (PS4 section).
Alternative for PS4_Strong: A statistically significant increase in cases over controls demonstrated by Fisher Exact or Chi-Squared analysis (PS4 section). The generic ACMG guidelines further specify this as a relative risk or odds ratio >5.0 where the confidence interval does not include 1.0 (Richards et al., 2015, Table 3, PS4 Note 1).
column26: One heterozygous proband (index case) with p.Ser269del (c.806_808delCCT) and autosomal dominant nonsyndromic genetic deafness was identified and sequenced (Figure 1A; DOCUMENT).
